title: Roblox Creators Earn Over $1B As Platform Drives Economic Growth Worldwide
text: From March 2024 to March 2025, Roblox creators earned over $1 billion globally, a year-over-year increase of more than 31%, through the DevEx Program. By September 2025, creator earnings on the platform surpassed $1 billion for the year, moving ahead of the $923 million recorded for all of 2024. The platform has seen dramatic growth in earnings for top creators. The top 10 developers earned an average of $33.9 million, and the top 100 developers earned an average of $6 million, up over 450% and 500%, respectively, since 2019. In 2024, the top 1,000 developers earned an average of $820,000, representing a 570% increase since 2019. Beyond individual earnings, Roblox has generated substantial macroeconomic impact. Between 2017 and 2024, Roblox contributed approximately 22,000 full-time job equivalents and $1.62 billion in GDP to the U.S. economy. In the same eight-year period, Roblox creators contributed $416 million in tax revenue. In just 2024 alone, Roblox generated a total GDP impact of $445 million, up 29% from 2023. Over the past eight years, 75% of payouts were made to creators in states without tech-focused economies. The platform has also expanded internationally, with Roblox's GDP impact in Indonesia reaching $8.2 million from 2019 to 2024.
